_meta.note: 24th major-arc enrichment dip; the LAST A-roundup unit (A2.5), and PASS 1 of an explicitly multi-pass mini-track. PASS-1 cut = Robb's Riverlands-relief rise: the Whispering Wood (Jaime captured) -> the Battle of the Camps (Riverrun relieved) -> the seam into robb-proclaimed-king-in-the-north. NO NEW NODES: the marquee battle already exists as the wiki-derived `battle-in-the-whispering-wood` (the baseline checked the wrong slug `battle-of-the-whispering-wood`, which is only a quarantined _conflicts twin; Lens C caught the live node). This is a WIRE + ENRICH dip converting that content-rich-but-edge-poor node from wiki-cited prose into queryable BOOK-cited edges. The whole battle cluster had only 2 causal edges (every hub causally islanded; only PRECEDES temporal links existed). HIGH VALUE = the relief-rise ENABLES spine (green-fork -> whispering-wood -> camps -> king-in-the-north; + siege-of-riverrun ENABLES camps via the three-camp dispersal). PLUS the whispering-wood shell node got its first 11 participant roles (robb COMMANDS_IN/AGENT_IN, grey-wind FIGHTS_IN, jaime VICTIM_IN, brynden-tully COMMANDS_IN [the screen], jon-umber/rickard-karstark/jason-mallister/theon-greyjoy FIGHTS_IN, maege-mormont AGENT_IN [her horn sprang the trap], LOCATED_AT whispering-wood). PLUS Jaime's final charge as 3 KILLS dyads + the 3 casualties VICTIM_IN (Torrhen Karstark, Eddard Karstark, Daryn Hornwood -- the seed of Rickard Karstark's vengeance). PLUS the gawen-westerling capture (a PASS-2 seam: it brings Robb to the Crag and the Jeyne-Westerling marriage blunder). WHODUNIT HONEST: the Whispering Wood + Camps are clean military victories; Lens B proposed ZERO SUSPECTED_OF (the deliberate-Bolton-sacrifice question is Duskendale = PASS 3). THREE pre-existing BUGGY edges flagged for bug_drop at finalize (confirmed against cat-09/10): `roose-bolton CAPTURES jaime-lannister` (Roose commanded the Green-Fork foot host on the east bank, cat-09:257 -- nowhere near the Wood), `catelyn-stark CAPTURES jaime-lannister` (she waited on the ridge and RECEIVED Jaime already taken, cat-10:101/117 -- a custody order, not a capture), and `robb-stark PRISONER_OF jaime-lannister` (inverted dup -- the correct `jaime-lannister PRISONER_OF robb-stark` already exists). The correct capture is now reified: jaime VICTIM_IN + robb AGENT_IN/COMMANDS_IN. NO TWOW. THEORY-GATED clean. PASS 2 = the Westerlands raid (Oxcross/the Crag/Jeyne-Westerling); PASS 3 = the unravelling (the Fords/Duskendale -> RW-upstream).

edges.quote: he'll march north to engage our main host, leaving our riders free to hurry down the west bank to Riverrun
edges.note: THE SPINE (1/4). Robb's stated plan at Moat Cailin: split the host -- foot + Bolton down the kingsroad as a feint to draw Tywin north (the Green Fork engagement), freeing the cavalry to fall on Jaime at Riverrun. ENABLES not CAUSES: Tywin chose to march, Robb chose to spring the trap. The Green-Fork diversion opened the window; the Whispering Wood is Robb's free act in it. (battle-on-the-green-fork PRECEDES battle-in-the-whispering-wood already exists -- that's temporal; this is the causal precondition.)

edges.quote: You may have lopped the head off the snake, but three quarters of the body is still coiled around my father's castle
edges.note: THE SPINE (2/4) -- the strongest link. Catelyn's metaphor: capturing Jaime (the head) leaves the besieging host (the body) leaderless and blind (his outriders/scouts already eliminated, cat-10:31), which the Battle of the Camps then destroys. ENABLES not CAUSES: Robb still had to choose and execute the camps assault.

edges.quote: Word of the victory at Riverrun had spread to the fugitive lords of the Trident, drawing them back
edges.note: THE SPINE (3/4). The twin victories + the relief of Riverrun re-assembled the northern AND fugitive river lords at the Riverrun war council where Robb was proclaimed. `execution-of-eddard-stark CAUSES robb-proclaimed-king-in-the-north` already exists (S113) -- that supplies the grievance/motive; this ENABLES supplies the military precondition that gathered the audience. Both coexist; the proclamation was the lords' free choice -> ENABLES.

edges.quote: There is no other way to besiege Riverrun, yet still, that will be their undoing
edges.note: THE SPINE (4/4) -- lights the causally-islanded siege-of-riverrun node. The Blackfish: the rivers force the besiegers into three separate camps, and that geometry 'will be their undoing.' The siege's dispersal IS the precondition that makes the sequential Camps assault possible. ENABLES (precondition), Tier-3 (structural). Distinct from E2 (E2 = leaderlessness; E4 = geometry).

edges.quote: When he comes after you, we will be waiting
edges.note: Robb designed and commanded the ambush, laying the trap on the Blackfish's map ('Raid him here ... we will be waiting -- here.'). The whispering-wood node had ZERO participant roles before this dip.

edges.quote: He moved away from her at a trot, leading his men downhill
edges.note: Robb personally led the cavalry charge down into the valley ('Winterfell!' he shouted as the arrows flew). Dual role with COMMANDS_IN: he both planned and fought (returned with his glove black with blood, cat-10:97).

edges.quote: she heard his direwolf, snarling and growling, heard the snap of those long teeth, the tearing of flesh
edges.note: Grey Wind fought in the wood and directly caused casualties (Theon's account, cat-11:117: 'I saw him tear one man's arm from his shoulder'). The rumor at Casterly Rock was that the wolf tore out Jaime's throat.

edges.quote: Between them they dragged Ser Jaime Lannister. They threw him down in front of her horse
edges.note: Jaime captured -- the defining outcome of the battle. This is the correct reification of the capture (replacing the buggy roose/catelyn CAPTURES dyads dropped at finalize).

edges.quote: Robb had given the Blackfish three hundred picked men, and sent them ahead to screen his march
edges.note: The Blackfish commanded the 300-man screen that blinded Jaime's outriders ('those that saw us did not live to tell of it') and led the Tully-banner lure that drew Jaime into the valley -- the tactical linchpin of the trap.

edges.quote: came the answer from the far ridge as the Greatjon winded his own horn
edges.note: The Greatjon (Jon Umber) held the far/west ridge, answered Maege's signal-horn, and led his riders down into the valley (Catelyn sees the moonlight on his line of lances 'like a thousand willowisps').

edges.quote: Lord Karstark's warhorns added their own deep, mournful voices to the dark chorus
edges.note: Karstark sealed the northern choke-point where the valley narrowed. His two sons Torrhen and Eddard died here at Jaime's hand -- the seed of the Karstark rupture that later costs Robb half his army.

edges.quote: Here was the call of Maege Mormont's warhorn, a long low blast that rolled down the valley from the east, to tell them that the last of Jaime's riders had entered the trap
edges.note: Maege Mormont's warhorn from the east ridge was the trigger that sprang the trap once all Jaime's riders had entered the valley -- the battle's opening act -> AGENT_IN.

edges.quote: Lord Jason Mallister had brought his power out from Seagard to join them
edges.note: Jason Mallister joined Robb's host before the battle with his Seagard power and held a flank of the valley (the node prose places him east with Maege; the Mallister trumpets blow in the trap, cat-10:83). Tier-2: he joined/fought but no individual combat line.

edges.quote: with Theon and the Greatjon at their head
edges.note: Theon rode in Robb's battle guard (cat-10:53) and is at the head of the victorious fighters dragging Jaime up the slope; the node's wiki prose states 'Theon Greyjoy ... kills men in the battle' (ACOK). Tier-2 -- present and fighting; book combat-act line is thin.

edges.quote: a thick carpet of dead leaves underfoot, densely wooded ridges sloping gently down to the streambed
edges.note: The battle is set in the forested valley of the Whispering Wood. The node had no live LOCATED_AT edge (only wiki prose).

edges.quote: he took Torrhen's hand off
edges.note: During his final charge to reach Robb, Jaime slew three of Robb's guard. Torrhen Karstark died of the wound (Robb confirms the dead at cat-10:129: 'Torrhen and Eddard. And Daryn Hornwood as well.').

edges.quote: He mislaid his sword in Eddard Karstark's neck
edges.note: Jaime killed Eddard Karstark in his charge -- the second of Rickard Karstark's two sons to die in the wood (the grief that later drives Rickard to murder the Lannister boy hostages).

edges.quote: split Daryn Hornwood's skull open
edges.note: Jaime killed Daryn Hornwood (heir to Hornwood) in his charge. daryn-hornwood DIED_AT whispering-wood already exists; this is the killer-dyad.

edges.quote: he took Torrhen's hand off
edges.note: Torrhen Karstark fell in the battle (Jaime's charge). Reification role -- 'who died in the Whispering Wood' (distinct from the KILLS dyad).

edges.quote: He mislaid his sword in Eddard Karstark's neck
edges.note: Eddard Karstark fell in the battle. Reification role on the event hub.

edges.quote: split Daryn Hornwood's skull open
edges.note: Daryn Hornwood fell in the battle. Reification role on the event hub.

edges.quote: Lord Westerling, Lord Banefort, Ser Garth Greenfield, Lord Estren, Ser Tytos Brax
edges.note: PASS-2 SEAM: Lord Gawen Westerling was captured at the Whispering Wood (named among the dozen captive lords in Theon's accounting). His capture is the thread that later brings Robb to the Crag and the Jeyne-Westerling marriage blunder (B1 Red-Wedding-upstream). Tier-2: node resolution ('Lord Westerling' = gawen-westerling). verify.
